title: Destructuring patterns should not be empty
description: Destructuring patterns should not be emptyAnalyze your codeintentionality-completereliabilityBugWhy is this an issue?More InfoDestructuring is a convenient way of extracting multiple values from data stored in (possibly nested) objects and arrays. It can make code more concise and expressive by directly extracting values or properties needed from arrays or objects. However, it is possible to define an empty pattern that has no effect, where no variables are bound to the destructured values.let {a: {}} = myObj; // Noncompliant: this does not create any variable function foo({p: []}) { // Noncompliant: this does not define any parameter // ..
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Empty destructuring patterns should be avoided as they create no variables
